steps.title: Find the defining clue in the passage
steps.content:
The passage says the groups that heard healthy reef sounds "were twice as likely as other groups to show signs of making a permanent home." The blank must describe larvae that reacted strongly and positively to environmental sound cues. The word needs to capture this stimulus-response behavior.
  • Choice A — 'responsive': means reacting readily to a stimulus — exactly what the larvae did when healthy reef sounds increased their likelihood of settling. ✓
  • Choice B — 'inactive': means not engaging in activity, which contradicts the larvae doubling their rate of reef-building behavior. ✗
  • Choice C — 'liked': describes being regarded with pleasure by others — not a logical description of a group's reaction to sound. ✗
  • Choice D — 'distressed': means experiencing difficulty or decline, which contradicts the positive outcome of increased settling behavior. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only "responsive" logically describes larvae that reacted to a sound stimulus by significantly increasing a specific behavior. That matches choice A — A is correct.
